class.name: CustomDecryptor
class.brief_description: Base class for implementing custom decryption logic for non-standard encrypted PCK files.
class.description: [CustomDecryptor] is the base class you extend to support games that use a non-standard encryption scheme for their PCK files. GDRE's built-in decryption handles the standard AES-256-CFB scheme used by Godot. If the game applies additional transformations to the key, uses a different cipher, or structures its encrypted file header differently, implement [method _parse_and_decrypt] in a GDScript subclass. Register the subclass with GDRE using [method GDRESettings.set_custom_decryption_script] (passing a script path) or [method GDRESettings.set_custom_decryptor] (passing an instance). You can also pass [code]--custom-decryption-script=<path>[/code] on the command line. [b]Warning:[/b] write a custom decryptor only after confirming the game genuinely does not use standard Godot encryption. Reverse-engineer the game binary to verify the encryption scheme first. [b]Warning:[/b] do not include encryption keys, XOR keys, or other secrets in a decryptor script you intend to distribute. Distributing such secrets may violate the DMCA in the United States and similar laws elsewhere. Most Godot encrypted files follow this layout: [codeblock] [magic: 4 bytes] # only present if non_pack_file == true [md5: 16 bytes] # MD5 of the decrypted data [length: 8 bytes] # int64, length of the original (unpadded) data [iv: 16 bytes] # initialization vector [data: length bytes, padded to 16-byte alignment] [/codeblock]

method.name: _parse_and_decrypt
method.description: Virtual method to override. GDRE calls this for each encrypted file it needs to read. [param file] is a [FileAccess] handle positioned at the start of the encrypted file data. When the file is stored inside a PCK, [code]file.get_length()[/code] returns the length of the entire PCK, not just this file's data. [param key] is the decryption key provided by the user or CLI. [param non_pack_file] is [code]false[/code] when the file is a slice inside a PCK (no magic number prefix) and [code]true[/code] when it is a standalone encrypted file that starts with a magic number. The return [Dictionary] must contain the following keys: - [code]error[/code] ([Error]): [code]OK[/code] on success, or an error code. - [code]length[/code] ([int]): length of the decrypted data in bytes (not padded). - [code]data[/code] ([PackedByteArray]): the decrypted bytes. [code]data.size()[/code] must equal [code]length[/code].

method.name: parse_and_decrypt
method.description: Non-virtual wrapper that invokes [method _parse_and_decrypt]. Use this when calling the decryptor from other GDScript code.
